just_starting.label: Start tonight
just_starting.consequence: There are important gaps in your awareness of how children use AI at home. Start with the guidance below and talk openly with your child about their AI use.

developing.label: Close the gaps
developing.consequence: You have a reasonable awareness of how your child uses AI but some important areas need attention at home — particularly homework boundaries and online safety risks.

aware.label: Growing awareness
aware.consequence: You have some awareness of AI risks, but there are still areas where more guidance would help you support your child safely.

confident.label: Confident
confident.consequence: You have a solid foundation for guiding your child's AI use. Keep conversations going and revisit the focus areas below as tools and risks evolve.

well_prepared.label: Well prepared
well_prepared.consequence: You are well placed to support safe, honest and independent AI use at home. Share what works with your child's school to help other families.

motif.default: Ask them to explain it back

headline.just_starting: Start with one calm conversation about how AI is used at home and for homework.
headline.developing: You have a base to build on. Clearer home boundaries will make AI use safer.
headline.aware: You understand some AI risks. Keep strengthening homework routines and online safety conversations.
headline.confident: Your home AI support is solid. Keep conversations regular as tools and school expectations change.
headline.well_prepared: You are well placed to support responsible AI use at home and share what works with school.

next_action.parent_awareness: Ask which AI tools they use

low.summary: You may not yet have a clear picture of how your child uses AI at home. Start with an open conversation about which tools they use and what for.
actions: Ask which AI tools they use on phones, laptops or at school
Find out whether they use AI for homework, games or social content
Agree to check in again after a week of noticing their habits

attention.summary: Your awareness of your child's AI use could be stronger. A short, calm check-in will help you spot gaps early.
actions: Ask them to show you one AI tool they use most often
Talk about what they would do if something online did not feel right

low.summary: Find out your school's expectations for AI and homework. Schools need to know how AI is used at home to support pupils fairly.
actions: Ask your child's teacher what the school's AI rules are
Check whether the school has shared parent guidance on AI
Agree how you will raise concerns if homework expectations are unclear

attention.summary: Your link with school expectations could be stronger. A shared understanding helps your child use AI safely for homework.
actions: Read any AI or homework guidance the school has sent home
Ask one teacher how families should support safe AI use

low.summary: Deepfakes are AI-generated images, videos or audio that look real but are fake. They can be used to bully, manipulate or scam — and young people are increasingly targeted. Most parents aren't yet aware of this risk.
actions: Talk to your child about what deepfakes are and show them an example
Agree that if something looks wrong they come to you first, not respond
Know your school's reporting process for online safety incidents

attention.summary: Your awareness of deepfakes and AI-generated harm could be stronger. Talk with your child about what to do if something online does not feel right.
actions: Ask your child if they have seen anything online that looked fake
Agree a simple rule: come to you before sharing or responding

low.summary: It may not be clear at home what AI your child can and can't use for schoolwork. Without a shared understanding, AI can become a shortcut that gets in the way of your child's learning — and could break school rules.
actions: Ask your child what AI rules their school has for homework
Agree a simple rule at home — try yourself first, then use AI to check
If unsure what's allowed, contact the school for their AI guidance

attention.summary: Homework oversight at home could be stronger. Clear boundaries help your child learn with AI rather than rely on it.
actions: Ask your child to explain one AI-assisted answer in their own words
Check your school's homework and AI expectations together

low.summary: Using AI to do homework for your child can hide gaps in learning. Model trying first, then using AI to check or explain — not to produce the answer.
actions: Try supporting without AI first when homework is hard
Use AI to explain concepts, not to write submissions for your child

attention.summary: Talk about privacy, fake images and voice messages, and agree what your child should do if something online does not feel right.
actions: Discuss what personal information should never be shared with AI tools
Agree who your child should tell if something online feels wrong
